observation: OperationalGotchas is the first always_loaded Scribe mode — a new third serving mode alongside observational and corpus. always_loaded means all tablets are injected into the K520.5 substrate cache at session start without explicit query. Implementation: registry.yaml mode field + readGotchasForCache() in writeSubstrateCache(). A&A #2310 reduction-to-practice for 'persistent consultation result' claim.

TypeScript mode union extended in two places: registry.ts ScribeEntry.mode and consult.ts ConsultResult.scribes_consulted[].mode + KnightScribesRegistry.scribes[].mode. always_loaded Scribes participate in consult_scribes retrieval (treated as corpus-mode for retrieval purposes) AND get injected into substrate cache. Two orthogonal behaviors; registry flag controls both.

13 seed tablets curated from B127 Bishop scaffold (OG-001..OG-011) + 2 new frictions discovered in K520.5 (OG-012: PowerShell BOM breaks Python json.loads; OG-013: Windows VM clock skew between PowerShell datetime arithmetic and Python time.time()). The 'Long Haul AND Fix Along the Way' constraint made K520.5 frictions directly available for K520.6 seeding. Both OG-012 and OG-013 have recurrence_count: 1 (first capture; will increment on future sessions).

promote_to_gotchas MCP tool reads Toolsmith JSONL by toolsmith_id, maps what_fails→friction and what_works→workaround, assigns next OG-NNN id, appends to OperationalGotchas JSONL. This creates a promotion pathway: friction discovered in any session → Toolsmith entry → after 3+ recurrences → promote_to_gotchas → enters always-loaded substrate cache. The full pipeline from discovery to always-loaded is now tooled.

writeSubstrateCache() now injects gotchas: readGotchasForCache() array alongside briefing text. Cache file structure at ~/.lb-session/substrate_cache.json now has three top-level fields: ts (Unix epoch), session_task, briefing (MoneyPenny output), gotchas (always-loaded OG tablets). Pre-K520.6 hooks that only check ts field are unaffected. Future hook improvements can read gotchas field directly without re-querying MCP.

Phase C verification required node --input-type=module --eval pattern (not temp .mjs files from temp directory) because ESM imports using relative paths resolve relative to the current file location, not CWD. Temp files in %TEMP% can't import './dist/scribes/cathedral.js' — the path resolves relative to %TEMP% not librarian-mcp/. Fix: run all node commands from librarian-mcp/ directory with --input-type=module --eval inline.

Phase C verification tests appended OG-014 (C.2 test tablet) and OG-015 (C.3 promote test) to scribe_OperationalGotchas.jsonl to verify the tools work. These test tablets were removed after verification by re-writing the file with only lines where og_id numeric <= 13. This is an exception to the append-only rule: test artifacts should not pollute the always-loaded Gotchas corpus. Production usage (add_gotcha, promote_to_gotchas) is append-only.

K520.6 close: 8/8 verification checks pass. New mode 'always_loaded' added to Scribe type system. 13 seed OG tablets committed. 3 MCP tools live: consult_gotchas (read all), add_gotcha (append new), promote_to_gotchas (Toolsmith→OG). substrate_cache.json gains gotchas[] field. TypeScript compiles clean. Files: registry.yaml, registry.ts, consult.ts, server.ts (modified); scribe_OperationalGotchas.jsonl (created); synapse_K520.6.jsonl, REPORT_KNIGHT_K520.6_B127.md (created).
